headline: Intel Chip-Factory Buyback Brings Apollo a Payday
summary: Intel has agreed to repurchase the 49% stake in its Ireland-based microchip factory it sold to Apollo Global Management for $14.2 billion.  The buyback gives Intel the full benefit of revenue from the factory as AI-driven demand for advanced microchips sends pricing higher.  It also gives a big windfall to Apollo and the debt investors who backed the deal.

raw.headline: Intel Chip-Factory Buyback Brings Apollo a Payday

The buyback gives Intel the full benefit of revenue from the factory as AI-driven demand for advanced microchips sends pricing higher.  It also gives a big windfall to Apollo and the debt investors who backed the deal.

headline: Updated / Change in the Supervisory Board of Apollo Group OÜ

headline: Intel Stock Gains After Buying Back Stake in Fab. Why the Market Is Excited.
summary: Intel  has agreed to buy back full control of its massive chip factory in Ireland through the repurchase of  Apollo Global Management’s  stake in the venture, sending its stock higher.  Intel stock gained 6.9% to $47.18 following the announcement.  Intel owned the remaining 51% and maintained full ownership and operational control of Fab 34 and its assets.

Intel stock gained 6.9% to $47.18 following the announcement.  Intel owned the remaining 51% and maintained full ownership and operational control of Fab 34 and its assets.

headline: Intel to Buy Back Apollo’s Stake in Ireland Chip Factory for $14.2 Billion. Why the Stock Is Gaining.
summary: Intel  has agreed to buy back full control of its massive chip factory in Ireland through the repurchase of  Apollo Global Management’s  stake in the venture, sending its stock higher.  Intel stock gained 6.9% to $47.18 following the announcement.  In 2024, Apollo-managed funds and affiliates led an $11.2 billion investment to acquire a 49% stake in a joint venture related to the Ireland facility, dubbed Fab 34.

raw.headline: Intel to Buy Back Apollo’s Stake in Ireland Chip Factory for $14.2 Billion. Why the Stock Is Gaining.

Intel stock gained 6.9% to $47.18 following the announcement.  In 2024, Apollo-managed funds and affiliates led an $11.2 billion investment to acquire a 49% stake in a joint venture related to the Ireland facility, dubbed Fab 34.

headline: Intel to buy back Apollo stake in Ireland factory for $14.2 billion
summary: Intel said it would spend $14.2 billion to buy back the 49% stake it had sold to Apollo Global Management in ‌its Ireland manufacturing facility, taking full ownership of the plant as its ‌finances improve and AI drives demand for its processors.  Apollo had paid $11.2 billion in 2024 to acquire the stake in a joint venture related to the plant in the town of Leixlip outside Dublin, giving the then-struggling Intel a cash infusion to fund its manufacturing expansion in Europe and the ‌United States.  Intel has since changed ⁠CEOs, with current top boss Lip-Bu Tan pursuing an aggressive restructuring to repair the company's finances, including job cuts and asset ⁠sales.

raw.headline: Intel to buy back Apollo stake in Ireland factory for $14.2 billion
raw.summary: Intel said it would spend $14.2 billion to buy back the 49% stake it had sold to Apollo Global Management in ‌its Ireland manufacturing facility, taking full ownership of the plant as its ‌finances improve and AI drives demand for its processors.  Apollo had paid $11.2 billion in 2024 to acquire the stake in a joint venture related to the plant in the town of Leixlip outside Dublin, giving the then-struggling Intel a cash infusion to fund its manufacturing expansion in Europe and the ‌United States.  Intel has since changed ⁠CEOs, with current top boss Lip-Bu Tan pursuing an aggressive restructuring to repair the company's finances, including job cuts and asset ⁠sales.

headline: Apollo Funds Acquire Gatehouse Living Group from Gatehouse Bank
summary: NEW YORK, April 01, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Apollo (NYSE: APO) today announced that Apollo-managed funds have acquired Gatehouse Living Group (“GLG” or “the Group”), a vertically integrated UK residential investment and management platform, from Gatehouse Bank (“GHB”). Financial terms of the transaction were not disclosed.
